name: pitfalls
description: Common ESP32 MicroPython errors → ranked fixes. AI can upvote/downvote fixes based on real device results. Highest (upvotes - downvotes) fixes are tried first. This is a self-healing knowledge base.

healing_mechanism.how_it_works: When AI applies a fix and it works, increment upvotes. When it fails, increment downvotes. Over time, the most reliable fixes per error/device/version float to the top.
healing_mechanism.how_to_vote: Edit this JSON: increment upvotes or downvotes on the fix you tried. Add device/version info to 'confirmed_on' or 'failed_on'.
healing_mechanism.vote_threshold: Fixes with (upvotes - downvotes) < -3 should be removed or marked deprecated.

MemoryError.description: Heap exhausted. Typical on ESP32 with ~160KB free.

fixes.action: Call gc.collect() before allocating large objects
fixes.when_to_use: Before creating large bytearrays, loading JSON, or opening sockets

fixes.action: Use bytearray pre-allocation instead of repeated allocations
fixes.code:
buf = bytearray(1024)  # allocate once, reuse
# instead of: buf = bytes(1024) in a loop
fixes.when_to_use: When reading sensor data or network packets in a loop

fixes.action: Stream large data instead of loading into memory
fixes.code:
import urequests
r = urequests.get(url)
for chunk in r.iter_content(512):  # 512 bytes at a time
    process(chunk)
fixes.when_to_use: Downloading files > 10KB or reading large JSON APIs
After finishing with a temporary large object

OSError: [Errno 12] ENOMEM.description: Out of socket memory. Too many open sockets or socket buffers too large.

fixes.code:
s = socket.socket()
try:
    # ... use socket ...
finally:
    s.close()
fixes.when_to_use: Always. Never rely on garbage collection for sockets.

OSError: [Errno 110] ETIMEDOUT.description: I2C/SPI/UART operation timed out. Bus hang or device not responding.
OSError: [Errno 110] ETIMEDOUT.quick_check: Check wiring, power, and pull-up resistors. Run i2c.scan() to verify.

fixes.code:
from machine import I2C, Pin
i2c = I2C(0, scl=Pin(22), sda=Pin(21))
# Or use SoftI2C which auto-recovers:
i2c = SoftI2C(scl=Pin(22), sda=Pin(21))
fixes.when_to_use: After I2C bus lockup. Hardware I2C needs re-init; SoftI2C handles this.

fixes.action: Check pull-up resistors (4.7kΩ on SDA/SCL to 3.3V)
fixes.code:
# Physical fix — no code change. Add 4.7kΩ resistors:
# 3.3V → [4.7kΩ] → SDA
# 3.3V → [4.7kΩ] → SCL
fixes.when_to_use: I2C works intermittently or fails after cable length > 10cm

WDT (Watchdog) Reset.description: CPU blocked for >5 seconds. Usually from wlan.connect() or a tight loop.
WDT (Watchdog) Reset.quick_check: Add print statements to find where code blocks.

fixes.action: Add time.sleep_ms(10) in long loops to feed watchdog
fixes.when_to_use: Any loop that runs longer than 1 second

fixes.action: Replace blocking wlan.connect() with non-blocking poll
fixes.code:
wlan.connect(ssid, password)
for _ in range(150):  # 15 second timeout
    if wlan.isconnected():
        break
    time.sleep_ms(100)  # feeds WDT
fixes.when_to_use: Startup WiFi connection or reconnection after signal loss

fixes.action: Disable watchdog for long operations (then re-enable)
fixes.code:
wdt = machine.WDT(timeout=30000)  # 30 second timeout
# ... long operation ...
wdt.feed()  # manually reset timer
fixes.when_to_use: Long flash writes, OTA updates, or large file transfers

ValueError: pin is input-only.description: GPIO 34-39 used as output or PWM. These pins are input-only by design.
ValueError: pin is input-only.quick_check: Check pin number against forbidden list.

fixes.action: Use a different GPIO pin (not 34-39) for output
fixes.code:
# GPIO 34-39: INPUT ONLY — ADC, touch, buttons
# GPIO 0-33: INPUT or OUTPUT — LEDs, servos, relays
# GPIO 6-11: FORBIDDEN — internal flash
fixes.when_to_use: Always check pin before assigning output/PWM

AttributeError: 'module' object has no a.description: Using CPython method that doesn't exist in MicroPython, or using deprecated 'u' prefix.
AttributeError: 'module' object has no a.quick_check: Check api/builtin-types.json for type methods, api/<module>.json for module functions.

fixes.code:
# Example: str.removeprefix() doesn't exist
# Check api/builtin-types.json → use startswith() + slicing
fixes.when_to_use: Any AttributeError on built-in types (str, bytes, int, float)

healing_example.scenario: User reports MemoryError when loading a 50KB JSON file
ai_workflow: 2. Try fix #1: gc.collect() — works, upvote it
3. Try fix #2: bytearray pre-allocation — also helps, upvote
4. Fix #3: streaming — json.loads() can't stream, so downvote (don't use for JSON parsing)
5. User reports code now works. Fixes #1 and #2 gain upvotes.
healing_example.next_time: AI sees upvoted fixes first, tries gc.collect() immediately, saves diagnostic time.
